# Fusion Framework Server

> Lambda-first TypeScript framework for AWS API Gateway. Decorator-based
> controllers (NestJS-style), tsyringe DI, use-case pattern, event
> listeners, and declarative permission checks.

This file is a curated index for LLM assistants. Read `llms-full.txt` for
the complete reference in one shot, or follow the links below for a
specific topic. The [README](./README.md) is the marketing-oriented
overview aimed at humans; `llms-full.txt` is the authoritative reference
for day-to-day coding.

## Critical conventions (read first)

These are the non-obvious rules that LLMs routinely get wrong. They are
intentional ŌĆö do not "fix" them by making the framework more permissive.

- **Path parameters MUST use `{id}`, NOT `:id`.** Fusion routes are
  matched against `event.resource` from API Gateway, which uses
  `{id}` notation. `:id` will silently fail to match. The framework
  will not add Express-style `:id` support; consistency across
  projects is the reason.
  - Correct: `@Get('/users/{id}')`, `@Delete('/orders/{orderId}/items/{itemId}')`
  - Wrong: `@Get('/users/:id')`
- **Decorator order matters.** `@Get('/x')` (or any HTTP decorator)
  is applied before `@Controller('/base')` rewrites the metadata.
  The HTTP decorator goes on the method, `@Controller` on the class.
  TypeScript applies them in bottom-up order ŌĆö this is the
  correct default, don't reorder.
- **Use cases are invoked via `UCExecutor`, never injected.** A class
  decorated with `@UseCase()` extends `UC` and is executed via
  `ucExecutor.execute(MyUC, ...args)`. Injecting a UC into another
  class's constructor is wrong.
- **Two `@Controller('/same-path')` classes are allowed.** Each gets a
  unique internal id. They only conflict if two methods register the
  exact same (httpMethod, resource) pair ŌĆö in which case the framework
  warns and last-registered wins.

## Decorators

- [HTTP decorators (`@Get`, `@Post`, `@Put`, `@Patch`, `@Delete`)](./src/core/decorators/http/)
- [`@Controller(basePath)`](./src/core/decorators/interfaces/controller.ts)
- [`@Listener(eventName | matchConfig)`](./src/core/decorators/interfaces/listener.ts)
- [`@UseCase()` + `UC` base class](./src/core/decorators/dependencies/use-case.ts)
- [`@Injectable(token?)`](./src/core/decorators/dependencies/injectable.ts)
- [`@Stage()` param decorator ŌĆö inject deployment stage](./src/core/decorators/stages/stage.ts)
- [`@RequirePermission(...perms, options?)`](./src/core/decorators/security/require-permission.ts)
- [`@ResolveAuth()` ŌĆö attach auth context without enforcing perms](./src/core/decorators/security/resolve-auth.ts)

## Core classes

- [`FusionResponse` ŌĆö response builder with chaining](./src/core/classes/fusion-response.ts)
- [`UC` / `UCExecutor`](./src/core/classes/)
- [`FusionException` + concrete subclasses (Validation, Unauthorized, ŌĆ”)](./src/core/exceptions/)

## Auth & permissions

- [`IAuthContextResolver` interface + `AUTH_CONTEXT_RESOLVER` token](./src/core/interfaces/auth-context.ts)
- [`DefaultCognitoAuthResolver` ŌĆö fallback when no resolver is registered](./src/core/classes/default-auth-resolver.ts)
- [`hasPermission` / `hasAllPermissions` / `hasAnyPermission`](./src/core/utils/permission-matcher.ts)
- [`getAuthContext(event)` ŌĆö read attached auth in handler](./src/core/decorators/security/current-user.ts)

## Event listeners (non-HTTP)

- [`EvtListener` interface](./src/core/interfaces/evt-listener.ts)
- [Pattern-matched listeners (`ListenerMatchConfig`)](./src/core/interfaces/listener-config.ts)
- [Pattern extraction helpers](./src/core/utils/pattern-matcher.ts)

## Runtime

- [`FusionServer.createHandler({ controllers, listeners?, cors? })`](./src/fusion-server.ts)
- Request routing: `event.httpMethod + event.resource` ŌåÆ controller method
- Listener routing: event-name first (O(1)), then first matching pattern

## Optional

- [Examples](./examples)
- [Tests ŌĆö useful as usage reference](./test)
